parts.text:
Create a data table in the style of a dark football statistics dashboard.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Dark navy/charcoal background (#1a1f2e). Alternating row shading between slightly lighter and darker navy.
    Columns: Season | Player | Club | Big Chances Created.
    Player names for repeat winners (De Bruyne, Fernandes, Salah) in a light blue accent color, others in white.
    The all-time record row (De Bruyne 2019/20, 33) should have the value highlighted in a green pill/badge with a 🔥 icon.
    Thin white horizontal dividers between rows. Column headers in muted grey, slightly smaller font.
    Title at top in white: 'Premier League — Big Chances Created leader per season'.
    Clean, no borders around the table, no background gradients. Looks like a premium stats website.

Create a vertical bar chart in the style of a dark football analytics dashboard.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Dark background (#111318). All bars in solid steel blue (#4a90d9). 
    Current/ongoing season bar (2025/26) in amber/gold (#f0b429) to distinguish it.
    X-axis shows seasons in short format (10/11, 11/12... 25/26). Y-axis shows values, starting from a cropped minimum (not zero).
    Display the exact value as a label above each bar in the same color as the bar.
    Thin horizontal gridlines in very dark grey — barely visible, just enough for reference.
    Title block at top left: small grey label 'PREMIER LEAGUE', then large white bold title 'Big Chances Created leader per season',
    then a grey subtitle: 'Each season · 2010/11 – 2025/26 | *Current season ongoing'.
    Legend at the bottom left: blue square = 'Completed seasons', gold square = 'Current season*'.
    No vertical gridlines, no bar borders, minimal padding.

Create a dark dashboard table with an inline horizontal bar embedded in each row.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Dark background (#13171f). Columns: Season | Player | Club | inline bar scaled to max value | value label.
    Inline bars in steel blue (#4a90d9), proportional to the max value (33). 
    The record row (De Bruyne 2019/20) bar in bright cyan (#00d4ff) with a subtle glow effect and a 'RECORD' tag.
    Repeat winners colored differently in player name column (light blue for De Bruyne, green for Fernandes, orange for Salah).
    Alternating row backgrounds, very subtle difference. Thin dividers. No outer border.
    Title: 'Premier League — Big Chances Created leader per season (2010/11–2025/26)'.

Create a horizontal bar chart as a compact dark dashboard widget.
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Very dark background (#0f1117). Slim horizontal bars in steel blue, left-aligned.
    Season label on the far left, player name + club inline beneath the season in smaller muted grey text.
    Value label at the end of each bar in white.
    Bars are thin and compact — this is a dense widget, not a large chart. Tight row spacing.
    The record bar (De Bruyne 2019/20, 33) is bright blue with a small 🏆 icon after the value.
    No axes, no gridlines, no tick marks. Purely the bars and labels.
    Title in small all-caps grey at top: 'PREMIER LEAGUE · BIG CHANCES CREATED LEADER BY SEASON'.

Create a dark leaderboard-style visualization split into two eras side by side: 2010/11–2017/18 (left) and 2018/19–2025/26 (right).
    Data (season — player (club) — big chances created):
    2010/11 — Didier Drogba (Chelsea) — 21
2011/12 — Robin van Persie (Arsenal) — 21
2012/13 — Leighton Baines (Everton) — 21
2013/14 — Luis Suárez (Liverpool) — 22
2014/15 — Cesc Fàbregas (Chelsea) — 16
2015/16 — Mesut Özil (Arsenal) — 29
2016/17 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 24
2017/18 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 19
2018/19 — Ryan Fraser (Bournemouth) — 28
2019/20 — Kevin De Bruyne (Man City) — 33
2020/21 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 20
2021/22 — Harry Kane (Tottenham) — 19
2022/23 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 32
2023/24 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 22
2024/25 — Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — 27
2025/26 — Bruno Fernandes (Man United) — 24
    Dark navy background (#161b27). Each era is a vertical table with columns: Season | Player | Value.
    Rows alternate between slightly lighter and darker shading.
    Player names for De Bruyne in light blue, Fernandes in green, Salah in orange, others in white.
    The highest value in each era gets a highlighted row (green tint, subtle) with a small badge.
    A thin vertical divider separates the two era columns.
    Era headers in muted uppercase grey: '2010s ERA' and '2020s ERA'.
    Main title centered at top in white bold: 'Premier League — Big Chances Created Leader Per Season'.
    Subtitle in grey: 'Min. one season winner shown per era'.
